title: To A Rose
content:
O rose! forbear to flaunt yourself,
        All bloom and dew - 
    I once, sad-hearted as I am,
        Was young as you.

    But, one by one, the petals fell
        Earthward to rot;
    Only a berry testifies
        A rose forgot.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Richard Le Gallienne, titled "To A Rose", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
